sessions.lever: explored 3 structural forms to demote s3/s4/s5; none beat floor 11

reviewer.evidence: No candidate adopted (no floor improvement). HEAD's 11 regfix rules (10 frame-offset substs + 1 insert nop) encode a register allocation GCC won't produce in pure C; no new cheat introduced.

remaining_gap.summary: Pure-C GCC 2.7.2 allocates 7 callee-saved regs (s0-s5,ra) and a -48 frame; target uses 4 (s0,s1,s2,ra) and -32. GCC over-promotes 3 long-lived values (&D_801027BC base, val1, an index temp) to s3/s4/s5 where target keeps them caller-saved. The 10 frame-offset substs + register renames correct this; the lone insert-nop is the separate maspsx .L-label load-delay blind spot (legitimately retirable via maspsx_label_nop_funcs.txt). The callee-saved over-promotion is the real blocker.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: 10 frame-offset substs (prologue idx 0-7, epilogue idx 59-63) correcting the -48->-32 frame + register allocation, plus 1 insert-nop (maspsx blind spot, retirable on its own via the allowlist).

next_hypotheses: Directed permuter from baseline targeting the s3/s4/s5 demotion (register allocation is the permuter's domain).
ALLOCDBG/-da RTL dump to identify which values GCC promotes to s3/s4/s5 and whether a lifetime-shortening restructure keeps them caller-saved.
If the promotion is immovable: canonical-asm authorization.

rejected_forms.form: inline the call args (drop idx1/val1 intermediates)
rejected_forms.reason: not a cheat; no change to the callee-saved promotion

rejected_forms.form: switch directly on D_800A38DC (drop val temp)
